quizzes.title: Project Planning & Scoping
quizzes.description: Reflection questions on project selection and planning

questions.question: Which factor is MOST critical when choosing a capstone project?
options: Choosing the most complex algorithms possible
questions.explanation: Data availability is critical - you can't build a model without data! Always verify data exists and is accessible before committing to a project.

questions.question: What makes a good Minimum Viable Product (MVP) for a capstone?
options: Core functionality working with room to expand
questions.explanation: A good MVP focuses on core functionality that works well. You can always add features later if time permits.

questions.question: You should leave deployment until Week 3 to focus on perfecting the model first.
questions.explanation: False! Deploy early (Week 2) to catch deployment issues early. Debugging deployment on the last day is stressful and risky.

questions.question: How many different ML techniques should your capstone project include at minimum?
options: 3+ techniques including supervised and (unsupervised OR deep learning)
questions.explanation: Minimum 3 techniques required, including supervised learning and at least one of unsupervised OR deep learning.

quizzes.title: Data Collection & EDA
quizzes.description: Questions about data quality and exploratory analysis

questions.question: What's the first thing you should do after collecting your dataset?
options: Perform thorough EDA to understand the data
questions.explanation: Always start with EDA! Understanding your data through visualization and statistics is crucial before modeling.

questions.question: What's a red flag in your dataset that could cause problems?
options: Data leakage (future information in training data)
questions.explanation: Data leakage is a critical issue that will make your model look great in training but fail in production. Always watch for future information leaking into training data.

questions.question: It's acceptable to use the test set to make decisions about feature engineering.
questions.explanation: False! Never touch the test set until final evaluation. Using it for decisions causes data leakage and inflated performance metrics.

quizzes.title: Model Development & Evaluation
quizzes.description: Questions about ML model development process

questions.question: What's the best approach when starting model development?
options: Start with the most complex deep learning model
Start with a simple baseline model, then iterate
Use the model that's most popular currently
Skip baseline and go directly to ensemble methods
questions.explanation: Always start with a simple baseline! It establishes minimum acceptable performance and helps you understand if complex models are actually helping.

questions.question: Your model achieves 95% accuracy on the test set. What should you check next?
options: Check confusion matrix and per-class metrics
questions.explanation: Accuracy alone can be misleading! Check the confusion matrix, precision, recall, and per-class performance to understand where the model succeeds and fails.

questions.question: Overfitting occurs when your model performs much better on training data than test data.
questions.explanation: True! Overfitting means the model has memorized the training data but doesn't generalize well to new data.

questions.question: What's the purpose of hyperparameter tuning?
options: To make the model more complex
To optimize model performance on validation data
To increase the size of the dataset
To reduce the number of features
questions.explanation: Hyperparameter tuning optimizes model configuration to achieve best performance on validation data.

questions.question: What should your API include for production readiness?
options: Just the prediction endpoint with no validation
questions.explanation: Production APIs need prediction endpoints, health checks, proper error handling, and input validation for reliability.

questions.question: It's fine to hardcode API keys and credentials in your GitHub repository.
questions.explanation: False! NEVER commit credentials to git. Use environment variables and .env files (in .gitignore) instead.

questions.question: What's the best way to handle missing values during prediction in production?
options: Use the same imputation strategy as training
questions.explanation: Apply the same preprocessing (including imputation) used during training to ensure consistency.

questions.question: What should a good README.md include?
options: Problem statement, features, installation, usage, architecture, performance, contact
questions.explanation: A comprehensive README should cover all aspects: problem, solution, setup, usage, architecture, metrics, and contact information.

questions.question: Code comments and docstrings are optional if the code is self-explanatory.
questions.explanation: False! Always document your functions with docstrings. What's obvious to you now may not be obvious to others (or future you).

questions.question: When creating a demo video, what's the ideal length?
options: As long as needed to show everything
questions.explanation: 2-3 minutes is ideal - long enough to show key features, short enough to keep viewer attention.

questions.question: How should you split time series data for training and testing?
options: Time-based split (train on past, test on future)
questions.explanation: Time series requires time-based splits to avoid looking into the future. Random splits cause data leakage.

questions.question: Feature scaling (normalization/standardization) should be fit on the entire dataset before splitting.
questions.explanation: False! Fit scaling only on training data to avoid data leakage. Then apply the same transformation to test data.

questions.question: What's the purpose of cross-validation?
options: To make the model train faster
To get more robust performance estimates
questions.explanation: Cross-validation provides more reliable performance estimates by testing on multiple different subsets of the data.

questions.question: A healthcare ML model with 95% accuracy is ready for clinical use.
questions.explanation: False! High accuracy alone doesn't mean clinical readiness. Need regulatory approval, extensive validation, safety studies, and proper disclaimers.

questions.question: If your model will impact people's lives (healthcare, finance, hiring), what's essential?
options: Explainability and transparency about how decisions are made
Using the most complex model available
questions.explanation: For high-stakes decisions, explainability is crucial. People deserve to understand how decisions affecting them are made.

questions.question: What should you include in a stock trading ML project?
options: Disclaimer that it's educational only and not financial advice
questions.explanation: Always include disclaimers for financial ML projects. You're not providing financial advice and past performance doesn't guarantee future results.

quizzes.title: Project Reflection
quizzes.description: Reflective questions about your capstone experience

questions.question: What was the biggest technical challenge you faced in your capstone project and how did you overcome it?
questions.guidance: Reflect on specific challenges (data issues, model performance, deployment problems) and the steps you took to solve them.

questions.question: If you were to start this project over, what would you do differently?
questions.guidance: Consider planning, technical decisions, time management, and scope.

questions.question: How did this capstone project integrate concepts from multiple modules of the course?
questions.guidance: Identify specific techniques from different modules and how they worked together in your project.

questions.question: What surprised you most during the development of your capstone?
questions.guidance: Could be technical surprises, time estimates, complexity, or insights gained.

questions.question: How will you explain this project to a potential employer in an interview?
questions.guidance: Practice your elevator pitch focusing on problem, solution, impact, and technical depth.

assessment_guidelines.open_ended: Graded on depth of reflection and learning demonstrated (0-3 points each)
